Acorns.summary: Acorns is barely in the news this period (2 stories). One positive roundup naming Acorns in the best robo-advisors of May, one neutral Q&A on whether you can pick individual stocks. No earned-media drumbeat right now.
Acorns.watch: Coverage volume is far below competitors. A proactive story angle could land easily — the space is wide open.

Kalshi.summary: Kalshi is everywhere this week (34 stories), but the story has two faces. Sports betting markets (NBA, Subway Series, even a Drake album guest-list market) are driving volume, while a federal lawsuit from four New Mexico tribes alleging illegal online sports betting on tribal lands is the dominant negative thread.
Kalshi.watch: Negative coverage is concentrated. The tribal lawsuit hit 7 outlets simultaneously — a real legal exposure story for a PR team to track.

Polymarket.summary: Polymarket coverage is more negative than positive (14 negative vs 7 positive). Esquire ran 'The Polymarket Has Turned America into a Deadly Casino,' the Israel/Iran war-betting angle is recurring, and Bloomberg covered Polymarket and Kalshi as the reason DraftKings just got a rare sell rating.
Polymarket.watch: Tone is hardening against prediction markets writ large — Kalshi gets pulled into the same narrative.

Chase.summary: JPMorgan Chase coverage is the broad-bank story shape: the new NYC headquarters tower opening, CEO transition speculation, and benchmark earnings analysis. Mostly neutral coverage with positive analyst notes, low negative volume.

Robinhood.summary: The dominant Robinhood thread is a new SEC filing for a second venture fund and continued crypto positioning. Coverage is mostly positive (8 pos, 5 neutral, 0 negative) — analysts framing them as more resilient than Coinbase among multi-asset platforms.

Cash App.summary: Cash App coverage skews positive on product usage but carries a recurring negative thread: local-news warnings about Cash App scams targeting consumers. Block's earnings remain the backbone.
Cash App.watch: Scam warnings hit local TV repeatedly — could erode trust signals over time.

SoFi.summary: SoFi coverage is heavily positive (7 of 8 stories). Analyst upgrades, member growth metrics, and lending-segment commentary lead the buzz. No negative coverage in the window.

Chime.summary: Chime coverage is split. Positive thread is IPO/secondary trading and neobank growth analysis. Negative thread is the recurring customer-complaint angle around account freezes and customer service.

Wealthfront.summary: Two stories, both neutral — fiscal Q1 2027 earnings date announcement plus a robo-advisor roundup. Quiet news cycle.

Betterment.summary: Two stories, both light coverage in robo-advisor 'best of' lists. Sentiment positive but volume low.

Greenlight.summary: One story this period — a positive mention in a financial-literacy roundup for kids. Below the news radar.

Alinea.summary: No press coverage in the last 14 days. Either pre-stealth, or actively avoiding media — worth a manual check.
